page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-32 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine configured with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. The system features 32 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 38 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI. System memory totals 128.0 GB, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server integrates a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, requiring external block storage solutions for persistent data. Network connectivity does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate highly specialized performance characteristics. The instance achieves top-tier results in large language model inference workloads for small and medium models. Specifically, prompt processing for a 135M parameter model reaches 11,863.44 tokens per second, while a 7B parameter model processes prompts at 2,231.74 tokens per second. Conversely, general-purpose CPU performance is more moderate. Single-core processing benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, register in the lower quartile, whereas multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth operations consistently fall within the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-32 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades off raw single-core CPU speed for accelerated graphics and tensor processing capabilities. This resource density makes it highly suitable for workloads such as medium-scale LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, image processing, and ray tracing. It is less optimal for single-threaded CPU-bound tasks or applications requiring high local storage throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-32 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 2.2 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. The hardware configuration includes a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, while local storage is absent. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division: single-core CPU execution and text processing are relatively weak, whereas multi-core operations and memory bandwidth perform at average levels. Most notably, the server delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, processing prompts for a 7B model at 2,231.74 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for GPU-accelerated workloads like machine learning inference, image processing, and ray tracing, rather than general single-threaded CPU tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-32 is a graphics-optimized server with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and one NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-32 features 32 Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for machine learning inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerated architecture combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with an NVIDIA L4 GPU for efficient machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1 NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM.
Features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory.
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference workloads.
No local storage is bundled, requiring external storage configurations.
Best suited for machine learning deployment, ray tracing, and image processing.
